blocks.content: Post-storm Millhaven was its own particular thing.

Porches that had been empty were filling up — people coming out to check the damage, which was never much. Mr. Whitaker was already in his front yard inspecting his garden with the expression of a general reviewing a successful campaign. He raised his hand at them without looking up.

They rode slow, because there was no reason not to. The streets were wet and the puddles were worth navigating around. The streetlights on Cedar had come on early, their sensors confused by how dark the storm had made the afternoon.
She looked at him sideways, then back at the road, and the corner of her mouth did the thing.
The light was changing fast now — the post-storm gold deepening toward orange, going faster than the regular afternoon.
Daisy shook her head once, slowly, in the way that meant she had already decided and was just letting him catch up.
